DSA.C Module 2 (Part 2 DS).pdf:
R e c u r s i o n
Recursion is a process where a function calls itself directly or indirectly.
 Used to solve problems that can be divided into smaller sub-problems.
 Must have a base condition to stop the recursion.
Example in daily life:
 A mirror placed in front of another mirror → repeated reflections.

DSA.MODULE 5 - REMAINING NOTE.pdf:
DATASTRUCTURE USING C                                                              Prepared by Dr. EBIN P M, AP - CSE AIML  
1 
 MODULE 5  
GRAPH  
• Graph is a nonlinear data structure.  
• Graph is shown as a tuple G=(V , E)  
                    V = set of vertex = {v₁, v₂, v₃,……. 𝑣_𝑛} 
                     E = set of edges = {e₁, e₂, e₃,……. 𝑒_𝑛} 
• Graph has no hierarchal relationship.  
• If a tree contains a closed loop or circuit,  it is a graph.  
• Tree is an acyclic graph . 
❖ Example

IPS.Experiment 6 _IPS.docx:
Experiment No. 6 

Aim: A university wants to automate result calculation for students. The system should take marks in 3 subjects, calculate total and average, and assign grades. Demonstrate the use of functions to perform repetitive tasks in a modular program. 
Create functions like:
get_marks()
calculate_total()
calculate_grade()
display_result()
Implement modular structure where main function calls other functions in sequence.

PEE.Comprehensive Guide to Basic Electrical:
Comprehensive Guide to Basic Electrical Engineering: From Theory to Concept  
Part 1: The Cast of Characters (Circuit Elements)  
1. Active vs. Passive Elements  
• The Concept:  Think of a circuit like a sound system.  
o Active Elements  are the "Boosters" (Amplifiers, Generators). They inject power 
or control the flow (like a faucet handle). Examples: Batteries, Transistors.  
o Passive Elements  are the "Consumers." They eat up energy (Resistors) or store 
it for later (Capacitors, Inductor
